perturbation.original_text: 1.5 "Development Fee" means 50% of the Net Sales for any Development Products in the Development Territory.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.5 "Development Fee" means 25% of the Net Sales for any Development Products in the Development Territory.
perturbation.explanation: Changing the Development Fee percentage from 50% to 25% creates a direct financial contradiction. This uncertainty affects the revenue split and enforcement of payments, specifically contradicting Section 2.1 which refers to the 'Development Fee' payment.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Subject to the terms and conditions of this Agreement, WPD hereby agrees to use its commercially reasonable efforts in good faith to take, or cause to be taken, all actions, and to do or cause to be done, all things necessary, proper or desirable or advisable under applicable laws to develop and commercialize the Development Products, with a goal of eventual approval of Development Products in the Development Territory. In exchange for the payment by WPD of the Development Fee to CNS, CNS hereby agrees to pay WPD the following payments: (i) within thirty Business Days from the date of this Agreement, CNS will make an upfront payment of $225,000 to WPD; and (ii) within thirty days of the verified achievement of the Phase II Milestone, (such verification shall be conducted by an independent third party mutually acceptable to the parties hereto), CNS will make a payment of $775,000 to WPD.
perturbation.justification: The original text defined the "Development Fee" as 50% of Net Sales. The changed text reduces this fee to 25%. The contradicted text refers to the payment of the "Development Fee" by WPD to CNS as consideration for CNS's agreement to pay WPD certain payments. Since the changed text alters the percentage of the "Development Fee", it directly contradicts the contradicted text, as the economic terms of the agreement are now inconsistent. The original percentage has been directly altered, creating a contradiction and making the agreement unclear.

perturbation.original_text: 2.1 Subject to the terms and conditions of this Agreement, WPD hereby agrees to use its commercially reasonable efforts in good faith to take, or cause to be taken, all actions, and to do or cause to be done, all things necessary, proper or desirable or advisable under applicable laws to develop and commercialize the Development Products, with a goal of eventual approval of Development Products in the Development Territory. In exchange for the payment by WPD of the Development Fee to CNS, CNS hereby agrees to pay WPD the following payments: (i) within thirty Business Days from the date of this Agreement, CNS will make an upfront payment of $225,000 to WPD; and (ii) within thirty days of the verified achievement of the Phase II Milestone, (such verification shall be conducted by an independent third party mutually acceptable to the parties hereto), CNS will make a payment of $775,000 to WPD.
perturbation.changed_text: 2.1 Subject to the terms and conditions of this Agreement, WPD hereby agrees to use its commercially reasonable efforts in good faith to take, or cause to be taken, all actions, and to do or cause to be done, all things necessary, proper or desirable or advisable under applicable laws to develop and commercialize the Development Products, with a goal of eventual approval of Development Products in the Development Territory. In exchange for the payment by WPD of the Royalty Fee to CNS, CNS hereby agrees to pay WPD the following payments: (i) within thirty Business Days from the date of this Agreement, CNS will make an upfront payment of $225,000 to WPD; and (ii) within thirty days of the verified achievement of the Phase II Milestone, (such verification shall be conducted by an independent third party mutually acceptable to the parties hereto), CNS will make a payment of $775,000 to WPD.
perturbation.explanation: Replacing 'Development Fee' with 'Royalty Fee' creates confusion because 'Royalty Fee' is not defined anywhere else in the contract. This contradicts the definition of 'Development Fee' in Section 1.5 and makes the reciprocal obligation between WPD and CNS unclear. The meaning of 'Development Fee' is clearly stated, yet is switched for an undefined term.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 1.5 "Development Fee" means 50% of the Net Sales for any Development Products in the Development Territory.
perturbation.justification: The original text clearly defines the 'Development Fee' as being paid by WPD to CNS. The changed text replaces 'Development Fee' with 'Royalty Fee,' which is not defined elsewhere in the contract and directly contradicts the existing definition of 'Development Fee' in Section 1.5, rendering the reciprocal obligations unclear. The contradicted text specifies that 'Development Fee' means 50% of Net Sales, which now clashes with the undefined 'Royalty Fee' mentioned in the changed text, creating an in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 6.2 Subject to any rights herein which survive termination, this Agreement will earlier terminate in its entirety: (i) upon thirty (30) calendar days written notice from either party if the other party materially breaches this Agreement, unless before the end of such thirty (30) calendar day notice period, the breaching party has cured the material default or breach to the non-breaching party's reasonable satisfaction; or (ii) at any time by mutual written agreement between the Parties, subject to any terms herein which survive termination.
perturbation.changed_text: 6.2 Subject to any rights herein which survive termination, this Agreement will earlier terminate in its entirety: (i) upon thirty (30) calendar days written notice from either party if the other party materially breaches this Agreement, unless before the end of such thirty (30) calendar day notice period, the breaching party has partially cured the material default or breach to the non-breaching party's reasonable satisfaction; or (ii) at any time by mutual written agreement between the Parties, subject to any terms herein which survive termination.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'cured the material default or breach' to 'partially cured the material default or breach' introduces ambiguity. It's unclear what constitutes a 'partial cure' and whether that's sufficient to avoid termination. This undermines the enforceability of the termination clause, as the original text requires the breach be cured fully, which is now up to interpretation.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Subject to any rights herein which survive termination, this Agreement will earlier terminate in its entirety: (i) upon thirty (30) calendar days written notice from either party if the other party materially breaches this Agreement, unless before the end of such thirty (30) calendar day notice period, the breaching party has partially cured the material default or breach to the non-breaching party's reasonable satisfaction; or (ii) at any time by mutual written agreement between the Parties, subject to any terms herein which survive termination.
perturbation.justification: The provided explanation states that changing 'cured the material default or breach' to 'partially cured the material default or breach' introduces ambiguity, making it unclear what constitutes a 'partial cure' and whether that's sufficient to avoid termination. However, the contradicted text is identical to the changed text, and both texts reflect the potential for 'partial cure.' Since there is no differing standard, there is no direct conflict or inconsistency. While the explanation suggests that ambiguity might arise, ambiguity alone is not a clear and direct contradiction. Therefore, according to the instructions to only assign 'YES' if there is a clear and direct conflict, inconsistency, or contradiction, the answer must be NO.
